---
output:
  word_document:
    reference_docx: report_template.dotx
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

# ==========================================================================================================
# FLYSHOOT Tripreport
# Updated: March 2026
#
# Sits in the same R/ folder as 00_setup.R – 03_main_workflow.R.
# Relies on config.json (written by 00_setup.R) and load_flyshoot_data()
# (defined in 02_storage_functions.R).
#
# All maps use geom_sf / sf objects — no geom_polygon / data.frame worlds.
# ==========================================================================================================

# rm(list = ls())  # removed: wipes calling environment when render() is used

# ==========================================================================================================
# PARAMETER HANDLING
# ==========================================================================================================

# Variables injected via envir= in render() call.
# Fallback defaults used when running chunks interactively in RStudio.
# Read render parameters passed via options() by the render script.
# options() is process-global and immune to environment scoping issues.
setvessel   <- getOption("flyshoot.vessel",      default = "SCH99")
startdate   <- getOption("flyshoot.startdate",   default = lubridate::dmy("17/02/2026"))
enddate     <- getOption("flyshoot.enddate",     default = lubridate::dmy("20/02/2026"))
lang        <- getOption("flyshoot.lang",        default = "nl")
max_species <- getOption("flyshoot.max_species", default = 12)

cat(glue("\n  ▶ Rendering vessel: {setvessel} | {format(startdate,'%d/%m/%Y')} – {format(enddate,'%d/%m/%Y')}\n"))

# ==========================================================================================================
# LANGUAGE STRINGS — all UI text goes through txt("key")
# ==========================================================================================================
strings <- list(
  nl = list(
    cap_samenvatting = "Samenvatting van de reis",
    cap_catch        = "Vangst per soort per dag (kg)",
    cap_tripmap      = "Overzicht van de reis en de vangsten per trek",
    cap_rectmap      = "Visserijlocaties per ICES rechthoek",
    cap_cumul        = "Cumulatieve aanvoer per soort, week en jaar",
    cap_haul_bar     = "Vangst per trek per soort",
    cap_bubble       = "Vangst per trek - kaart (totaal gewicht)",
    col_trip         = "Trip",
    col_from         = "Van",
    col_to           = "Tot",
    col_days         = "Dagen",
    col_hauls        = "Trekken",
    col_hauls_day    = "Tr/dag",
    col_distance     = "Afstand (nm)",
    col_weeks        = "Week",
    col_divisions    = "Divisie",
    col_rects        = "ICES rect",
    col_gear         = "Gear",
    col_mesh         = "Maaswijdte (mm)",
    col_vopening     = "Vert. opening (m)",
    col_cable        = "Kabel (m)",
    col_cablethick   = "Kabeldikte (mm)",
    col_groundrope   = "Grondtouw (m)",
    col_escape       = "Escape panel",
    col_species      = "Soort",
    col_code         = "Code",
    col_total_kg     = "Totaal (kg)",
    total_row        = "TOTAAL",
    source_label     = "Bron",
    cap_catch_overview = "Overzicht aanvoer en discards per reis",
    cap_catch_zone   = "Vangst per soort per economische zone (kg)",
    overall_discards   = "Totale discards per reis",
    lbl_aanvoer        = "aanvoer",
    lbl_vangst         = "totale vangst",
    col_source       = "Bron",
    col_presentation = "Presentatie",
    col_discard_rsn  = "Discard reden",
    col_weight_total = "Totaal (kg)",
    row_total        = "Totaal",
    row_elog         = "Elog",
    row_elog_trek    = "Elog trek",
    row_kisten       = "Kisten",
    hdr_report       = "Reisoverzicht",
    hdr_trip         = "Reis",
    hdr_start        = "Start",
    hdr_end          = "Einde",
    hdr_trips_for    = "reis voor vaartuig",
    hdr_generated    = "Rapport gegenereerd",
    plot_locs        = "Overzicht van de reis en de vangsten per trek",
    plot_trip_label  = "Reis:",
    plot_rect        = "locaties per ICES rechthoek",
    plot_cumul       = "cumulatieve aanvoer per soort",
    plot_haul        = "vangst per trek",
    plot_haul_kg     = "vangst per trek (kg)",
    plot_trip_lbl    = "Reis:",
    xlab_week        = "ISO week",
    ylab_cumul       = "Aanvoer (kg, cumulatief)",
    xlab_haul        = "Trek (haul_id)",
    ylab_weight      = "Gewicht (kg)",
    legend_species   = "Soort",
    legend_year      = "Jaar",
    legend_rect      = "ICES rect"
  ),
  en = list(
    cap_samenvatting = "Trip summary",
    cap_catch        = "Catch by species per day (kg)",
    cap_tripmap      = "Overview of the trip and catches per haul",
    cap_rectmap      = "Fishing locations by ICES rectangle",
    cap_cumul        = "Cumulative landings by species, week and year",
    cap_haul_bar     = "Catch per haul by species",
    cap_bubble       = "Catch per haul - map (total weight)",
    col_trip         = "Trip",
    col_from         = "From",
    col_to           = "To",
    col_days         = "Days",
    col_hauls        = "Hauls",
    col_hauls_day    = "Hauls/day",
    col_distance     = "Distance (nm)",
    col_weeks        = "Week",
    col_divisions    = "Division",
    col_rects        = "ICES rect",
    col_gear         = "Gear",
    col_mesh         = "Mesh size (mm)",
    col_vopening     = "Vert. opening (m)",
    col_cable        = "Cable (m)",
    col_cablethick   = "Cable diam. (mm)",
    col_groundrope   = "Groundrope (m)",
    col_escape       = "Escape panel",
    col_species      = "Species",
    col_code         = "Code",
    col_total_kg     = "Total (kg)",
    total_row        = "TOTAL",
    source_label     = "Source",
    cap_catch_overview = "Overview landings and discards per trip",
    cap_catch_zone   = "Catch by species per economic zone (kg)",
    overall_discards   = "Overall discards per trip",
    lbl_aanvoer        = "landings",
    lbl_vangst         = "total catch",
    col_source       = "Source",
    col_presentation = "Presentation",
    col_discard_rsn  = "Discard reason",
    col_weight_total = "Total (kg)",
    row_total        = "Total",
    row_elog         = "Elog",
    row_elog_trek    = "Elog trek",
    row_kisten       = "Kisten",
    hdr_report       = "Tripreport",
    hdr_trip         = "Trip",
    hdr_start        = "Start",
    hdr_end          = "End",
    hdr_trips_for    = "trip(s) for vessel",
    hdr_generated    = "Report generated",
    plot_locs        = "Overview of the trip and catches per haul",
    plot_trip_label  = "Trip:",
    plot_rect        = "locations by ICES rectangle",
    plot_cumul       = "cumulative landings by species",
    plot_haul        = "catch per haul",
    plot_haul_kg     = "catch per haul (kg)",
    plot_trip_lbl    = "Trip:",
    xlab_week        = "ISO week",
    ylab_cumul       = "Landings (kg, cumulative)",
    xlab_haul        = "Haul (haul_id)",
    ylab_weight      = "Weight (kg)",
    legend_species   = "Species",
    legend_year      = "Year",
    legend_rect      = "ICES rect"
  )
)
txt <- function(key) { v <- strings[[lang]][[key]]; if (is.null(v)) stop("Unknown key: ", key); v }
species_name_col <- if (lang == "nl") "dutchname" else "englishname"
tab_prefix <- if (lang == "nl") "Tabel " else "Table "
fig_prefix <- if (lang == "nl") "Figuur " else "Figure "

# ==========================================================================================================
# LIBRARIES
# ==========================================================================================================

library(withr)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(arrow)
library(sf)
library(jsonlite)
library(glue)
library(flextable)
library(officer)
library(captioner)
library(patchwork)
library(viridis)
library(icosa)
library(ggnewscale)  # allows two fill scales in same plot
# marmap and metR are optional — install for depth contours:
# install.packages(c('marmap','metR'))

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE,
                      comment = "", crop = TRUE, fig.width = 10)
options(dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE)

tab_nums <- captioner::captioner(prefix = tab_prefix, levels = 1, type = "n", infix = ".")
fig_nums <- captioner::captioner(prefix = fig_prefix, levels = 1, type = "n", infix = ".")

# flextable defaults — applied to every table in the document
flextable::set_flextable_defaults(
  font.family  = "Calibri",
  font.size    = 9,
  border.color = "#AAAAAA",
  padding      = 3,
  theme_fun    = theme_vanilla
)
# Left-align all flextables on the page (no centering)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(ft.align = "left")

# ==========================================================================================================
# PROJECT ROOT
#
# knitr knits in a fresh R session with a different working directory than your
# interactive RStudio session. Relative paths and here::here() therefore behave
# differently between the two contexts — the root cause of the path errors.
#
# Solution: store the project root in .Renviron so it is available identically
# in every context (interactive, knit, batch render, Task Scheduler).
#
# One-time setup:
#   1. Run:  usethis::edit_r_environ()
#   2. Add:  FLYSHOOT_PROJECT=C:/GIT/flyshoot
#            (use your actual project root — the folder containing config.json)
#   3. Save and restart R (Session > Restart R)
# ==========================================================================================================

project_root <- Sys.getenv("FLYSHOOT_PROJECT")

if (project_root == "") {
  stop(
    "Environment variable FLYSHOOT_PROJECT is not set.\n\n",
    "  1. Run:  usethis::edit_r_environ()\n",
    "  2. Add:  FLYSHOOT_PROJECT=C:/GIT/flyshoot\n",
    "           (replace with the folder that contains config.json)\n",
    "  3. Save, then restart R: Session > Restart R\n",
    "  4. Knit again."
  )
}

# Convenience wrapper — all file paths in this Rmd use p("subpath")
p <- function(...) file.path(project_root, ...)

# Word template — pandoc resolves reference_docx relative to the Rmd file, so
# copy it next to the Rmd once at the start of each knit session.
# This means report_template.dotx only needs to exist in the project root.
local({
  template_src <- p("report_template.dotx")
  rmd_input    <- knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE)  # NULL when run interactively
  if (!is.null(rmd_input) && file.exists(template_src)) {
    template_dest <- file.path(dirname(rmd_input), "report_template.dotx")
    if (!identical(template_src, template_dest))
      file.copy(template_src, template_dest, overwrite = TRUE)
  }
})

# Check whether the output .docx is already open in Word (Windows locks the
# file for writing). Catching this here gives a clear message instead of a
# cryptic pandoc "permission denied" error at the very end of knitting.
local({
  rmd_input <- knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE)
  if (!is.null(rmd_input)) {
    docx_out <- paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(rmd_input), ".docx")
    if (file.exists(docx_out)) {
      test <- tryCatch(
        { con <- file(docx_out, open = "ab"); close(con); FALSE },
        error = function(e) TRUE
      )
      if (test) stop(
        "Output file is open in Word — please close it before knitting:\n  ",
        docx_out
      )
    }
  }
})

# ==========================================================================================================
# CONFIGURATION — load from config.json written by 00_setup.R
# ==========================================================================================================

if (!file.exists(p("config.json"))) {
  stop("config.json not found at: ", p("config.json"), "\nPlease run 00_setup.R first.")
}
config <- jsonlite::read_json(p("config.json"))

# source() the functions files with the working directory temporarily set to
# the project root. This means the plain file.exists("config.json") checks
# inside those files resolve correctly, identically to running them interactively.
withr::with_dir(project_root, {
  source(p("R", "harmonized", "02_storage_functions.R"))
  source(p("R", "FLYSHOOT utils.R"))
})

# ==========================================================================================================
# SPATIAL DATA
# Loads the same sf objects that 03_main_workflow.R uses for spatial enrichment.
# All objects live under config$spatial_data.
# ==========================================================================================================

spatialdir <- config$spatial_data

# World coastline (medium resolution sf object)
load(file.path(spatialdir, "world_mr_sf.RData"))   # -> world_mr_sf

# ICES rectangles sf
rect_sf_path <- file.path(spatialdir, "rect_sf.RData")
if (file.exists(rect_sf_path)) {
  load(rect_sf_path)                               # -> rect_sf
  rect_sf <- rect_sf %>% dplyr::select(rect = ICESNAME)
} else {
  rect_sf <- NULL
}

# EEZ sf
eez_sf_path <- file.path(spatialdir, "eez.sf.RData")
if (file.exists(eez_sf_path)) {
  # Capture whatever object name is inside the file
  eez_before <- ls()
  load(eez_sf_path)
  eez_after  <- setdiff(ls(), eez_before)
  # Standardise to eez_sf regardless of stored name
  if (length(eez_after) > 0) {
    eez_sf <- get(eez_after[1])
    rm(list = eez_after[eez_after != "eez_sf"])
  } else {
    eez_sf <- NULL
  }
} else {
  # Try alternative common filenames
  alt_paths <- c(
    file.path(spatialdir, "eez.RData"),
    file.path(spatialdir, "eez_sf.RData"),
    file.path(spatialdir, "eez.df.RData")
  )
  found <- Filter(file.exists, alt_paths)
  if (length(found) > 0) {
    eez_before <- ls()
    load(found[1])
    eez_after  <- setdiff(ls(), eez_before)
    if (length(eez_after) > 0) {
      obj <- get(eez_after[1])
      # Convert to sf if it is a data frame (old eez.df format)
      eez_sf <- if (inherits(obj, "sf")) obj
                else if (is.data.frame(obj) &&
                         all(c("long","lat","group") %in% names(obj))) {
                  message("  ⚠ eez loaded as data.frame — EEZ boundaries not available as sf")
                  NULL
                } else obj
      rm(list = eez_after[eez_after != "eez_sf"])
    } else { eez_sf <- NULL }
  } else {
    message("  ⚠ No EEZ file found in ", spatialdir)
    eez_sf <- NULL
  }
}

# ASFIS species codes (flat table — not sf)
asfis <- loadRData(file.path(spatialdir, "asfis.RData")) %>%
  rename_with(tolower)

# ==========================================================================================================
# DATA LOADING — via load_flyshoot_data() from 02_storage_functions.R
# Filtered upfront to the requested vessel; trip filtering happens after
# trip_id resolution below.
# ==========================================================================================================

use_haul_data  <- TRUE
use_kisten_data <- TRUE
use_elog_data  <- TRUE
use_elog_trek  <- TRUE
use_trip_data  <- TRUE

safe_load <- function(type) {
  tryCatch(
    load_flyshoot_data(type, vessel_ids = setvessel),
    error = function(e) { message(glue("  ⚠ Could not load {type}: {e$message}")); tibble() }
  )
}

if (use_elog_data)   elog      <- safe_load("elog")
if (use_elog_trek)   elog_trek <- safe_load("elog_trek")
if (use_haul_data)   haul      <- safe_load("haul")
if (use_kisten_data) kisten    <- safe_load("kisten")
if (use_trip_data)   trip      <- safe_load("trip")
vm_raw <- tryCatch(
  load_flyshoot_data("vessel_movement", vessel_ids = setvessel),
  error = function(e) { message("  ⚠ vessel_movement not available"); tibble() }
)

# ==========================================================================================================
# TRIP RESOLUTION — derive trip_id(s) from date range
# ==========================================================================================================

# Primary: elog  |  fallbacks: haul, elog_trek, kisten
# NOTE: always filter on vessel AND date — trip_ids are not unique across vessels
resolve_settrip <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    dplyr::filter(vessel %in% setvessel, date >= startdate, date <= enddate) %>%
    dplyr::distinct(trip_id) %>%
    dplyr::pull(trip_id)
}

settrip <- if (use_elog_data && nrow(elog) > 0 &&
               length(resolve_settrip(elog)) > 0) {
  resolve_settrip(elog)
} else if (use_haul_data && nrow(haul) > 0 &&
           length(resolve_settrip(haul)) > 0) {
  resolve_settrip(haul)
} else if (use_elog_trek && nrow(elog_trek) > 0 &&
           length(resolve_settrip(elog_trek)) > 0) {
  resolve_settrip(elog_trek)
} else if (use_kisten_data && nrow(kisten) > 0 &&
           length(resolve_settrip(kisten)) > 0) {
  resolve_settrip(kisten)
} else {
  stop(glue("No data found for vessel {setvessel} between ",
            "{format(startdate,'%d/%m/%Y')} and {format(enddate,'%d/%m/%Y')}"))
}

if (length(settrip) == 0) {
  stop(glue("No trips found for vessel {setvessel} in requested date range."))
}
vm     <- if (nrow(vm_raw) > 0) dplyr::filter(vm_raw, vessel %in% setvessel, trip_id %in% settrip) else vm_raw
has_vm <- nrow(vm) > 0

# ==========================================================================================================
# FILTER ALL DATASETS TO settrip
# ==========================================================================================================

harmonise_species <- function(x) dplyr::case_when(x == "SQC" ~ "SQR", TRUE ~ x)

# Filter on BOTH vessel and trip_id — trip_ids alone are not unique across vessels
if (use_elog_data && nrow(elog) > 0) {
  e <- elog %>%
    dplyr::filter(vessel %in% setvessel, trip_id %in% settrip) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(species_code = harmonise_species(species_code))
} else { e <- tibble() }

if (use_elog_trek && nrow(elog_trek) > 0) {
  et <- elog_trek %>%
    dplyr::filter(vessel %in% setvessel, trip_id %in% settrip) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      shoot_lon    = dplyr::if_else(shoot_lon == 0 & shoot_lat == 0, NA_real_, shoot_lon),
      shoot_lat    = dplyr::if_else(is.na(shoot_lon), NA_real_, shoot_lat),
      species_code = harmonise_species(species_code)
    )
  # Fallback: trip_id mismatch between elog and elog_trek (pipeline bug)
  # e.g. elog has "2026031600015", elog_trek has "2026031620260319"
  # In that case filter by vessel + date range instead
  if (nrow(et) == 0) {
    message("  ⚠ elog_trek trip_id mismatch with elog — filtering by date range instead")
    et <- elog_trek %>%
      dplyr::filter(vessel %in% setvessel,
                    date >= startdate, date <= enddate) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        shoot_lon    = dplyr::if_else(shoot_lon == 0 & shoot_lat == 0, NA_real_, shoot_lon),
        shoot_lat    = dplyr::if_else(is.na(shoot_lon), NA_real_, shoot_lat),
        species_code = harmonise_species(species_code)
      )
    if (nrow(et) > 0)
      message(glue("  ✓ elog_trek: {nrow(et)} rows via date fallback | trip_ids: ",
                   "{paste(unique(et$trip_id), collapse=', ')}"))
  }
} else { et <- tibble() }

if (use_haul_data && nrow(haul) > 0) {
  h <- haul %>% dplyr::filter(vessel %in% setvessel, trip_id %in% settrip)
} else { h <- tibble() }

if (use_kisten_data && nrow(kisten) > 0) {
  m <- kisten %>%
    dplyr::filter(vessel %in% setvessel, trip_id %in% settrip) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      species_code = toupper(species_code),
      species_code = harmonise_species(species_code)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
} else { m <- tibble() }

if (use_trip_data && nrow(trip) > 0) {
  tt <- trip %>% dplyr::filter(vessel %in% setvessel, trip_id %in% settrip)
} else { tt <- tibble() }

# Availability flags
has_elog      <- nrow(e)  > 0
has_elog_trek <- nrow(et) > 0
has_haul      <- nrow(h)  > 0
has_kisten    <- nrow(m)  > 0

if (!has_elog && !has_elog_trek && !has_haul && !has_kisten) {
  stop(glue("No data for vessel {setvessel}, trip(s) {paste(settrip, collapse=',')}"))
}

# ==========================================================================================================
# SPECIES CONFIGURATION — top 10 by weight
# ==========================================================================================================

catch_for_species <- if (has_elog) e else if (has_kisten) m else tibble()

if (nrow(catch_for_species) > 0) {
  myspecies <- catch_for_species %>%
    dplyr::group_by(species_code) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(weight = sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(weight)) %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = max_species) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(
      dplyr::select(asfis, species, englishname, dutchname),
      by = c("species_code" = "species")
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(label = paste(englishname, species_code))
} else {
  myspecies <- tibble(species_code = character(), weight = numeric(),
                      englishname = character(), dutchname = character(), label = character())
}
nspecies <- nrow(myspecies)

# ==========================================================================================================
# SPATIAL SETUP — build sf point objects from position columns
# These are reused by all map chunks below.
# ==========================================================================================================

make_sf_points <- function(df, lon = "shoot_lon", lat = "shoot_lat") {
  df %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[lon]]), !is.na(.data[[lat]])) %>%
    sf::st_as_sf(coords = c(lon, lat), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)
}

# ICES rect centroid helper — used as position fallback when no shoot_lon/lat available
ices_rect_to_lonlat <- function(rects) {
  rects <- trimws(toupper(rects))
  valid <- grepl("^[0-9]{2}[A-Z][0-9]$", rects)
  lon   <- rep(NA_real_, length(rects))
  lat   <- rep(NA_real_, length(rects))
  # Exact mapplots::ices.rect formula (Hans Gerritsen)
  # rx = paste0(1-based letter index, digit) as integer; lon = rx - 59.5
  # lat = (row_num + 71.5) / 2
  if (any(valid, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    row_num  <- as.integer(substr(rects[valid], 1, 2))
    col_lett <- match(substr(rects[valid], 3, 3), LETTERS)   # 1-based: A=1,E=5,F=6...
    col_dig  <- substr(rects[valid], 4, 4)
    rx       <- as.integer(paste0(col_lett, col_dig))
    lon[valid] <- rx - 59.5
    lat[valid] <- (row_num + 71.5) / 2
  }
  data.frame(lon = lon, lat = lat)
}

# Derive approximate positions from ICES rect centroids when no actual positions exist
derive_positions_from_rects <- function(df) {
  rect_col <- intersect(c("ices_rect", "ices_rectangle"), names(df))
  if (length(rect_col) == 0) return(tibble::tibble())
  rects <- unique(na.omit(df[[rect_col[1]]]))
  if (length(rects) == 0) return(tibble::tibble())
  coords <- ices_rect_to_lonlat(rects)
  valid  <- !is.na(coords$lon) & !is.na(coords$lat)
  if (!any(valid)) return(tibble::tibble())
  tibble::tibble(shoot_lon = coords$lon[valid], shoot_lat = coords$lat[valid])
}

# Best position source for bounding box
pos_sf <- if (has_elog_trek && any(!is.na(et$shoot_lon))) {
  make_sf_points(et)
} else if (has_elog && any(!is.na(e$shoot_lon))) {
  make_sf_points(e)
} else if (has_haul && any(!is.na(h$shoot_lon))) {
  make_sf_points(h)
} else if (has_elog) {
  # Fallback: derive approximate positions from ICES rect centroids in elog
  rect_pts <- derive_positions_from_rects(e)
  if (nrow(rect_pts) > 0) {
    sf::st_as_sf(rect_pts, coords = c("shoot_lon", "shoot_lat"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)
  } else { NULL }
} else {
  NULL
}

# ==========================================================================================================
# MAP EXTENT
# Include fishing positions AND vessel_movement positions for the bounding box
# so port-to-port routes don't get clipped.
# ==========================================================================================================

# Collect all positions: haul data + vessel_movement route
# Collect real positions first
real_lons <- c(
  if (has_elog_trek) et$shoot_lon[!is.na(et$shoot_lon)],
  if (has_elog)       e$shoot_lon[!is.na(e$shoot_lon)],
  if (has_haul)       h$shoot_lon[!is.na(h$shoot_lon)]
)
real_lats <- c(
  if (has_elog_trek) et$shoot_lat[!is.na(et$shoot_lat)],
  if (has_elog)       e$shoot_lat[!is.na(e$shoot_lat)],
  if (has_haul)       h$shoot_lat[!is.na(h$shoot_lat)]
)

# Only derive rect-based positions when NO real positions exist
rect_approx_pos <- if (length(real_lons) == 0 && has_elog) {
  derive_positions_from_rects(e)
} else { tibble::tibble() }

# vm positions: include all (port + elog_rect haul positions) for a complete bbox
vm_lons <- if (has_vm) vm$lon[!is.na(vm$lon)] else c()
vm_lats <- if (has_vm) vm$lat[!is.na(vm$lat)] else c()

all_lons <- c(real_lons, vm_lons,
              if (nrow(rect_approx_pos) > 0) rect_approx_pos$shoot_lon)
all_lats <- c(real_lats, vm_lats,
              if (nrow(rect_approx_pos) > 0) rect_approx_pos$shoot_lat)

if (length(all_lons) > 0 && length(all_lats) > 0) {
  lon_range <- range(all_lons, na.rm = TRUE)
  lat_range <- range(all_lats, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (diff(lon_range) == 0) lon_range <- lon_range + c(-1, 1)
  if (diff(lat_range) == 0) lat_range <- lat_range + c(-0.5, 0.5)
} else {
  lon_range <- c(-2, 8); lat_range <- c(50, 57)
}

# Use tighter buffer for rect-based trips (centroids are already ~0.5deg from edge)
using_rect_positions <- length(real_lons) == 0 && nrow(rect_approx_pos) > 0
lon_buffer <- diff(lon_range) * if (using_rect_positions) 0.05 else 0.1
lat_buffer <- diff(lat_range) * if (using_rect_positions) 0.05 else 0.1
xmin <- floor(lon_range[1] - lon_buffer)
xmax <- ceiling(lon_range[2] + lon_buffer)
ymin <- floor((lat_range[1] - lat_buffer) * 2) / 2
ymax <- ceiling((lat_range[2] + lat_buffer) * 2) / 2

target_aspect <- 1.0
if (xmin == xmax) { xmin <- xmin - 0.5; xmax <- xmax + 0.5 }
if (ymin == ymax) { ymin <- ymin - 0.3; ymax <- ymax + 0.3 }
xdistance   <- icosa::arcdist(p1 = c(xmin, ymin), p2 = c(xmax, ymin))
ydistance   <- icosa::arcdist(p1 = c(xmin, ymin), p2 = c(xmin, ymax))
data_aspect <- if (is.finite(xdistance) && xdistance > 0) ydistance / xdistance else 1.0

if (data_aspect > target_aspect) {
  extra_x_deg <- (ydistance / target_aspect - xdistance) / 2 / 111
  xmin <- xmin - extra_x_deg
  xmax <- xmax + extra_x_deg
} else {
  extra_y_deg <- (xdistance * target_aspect - ydistance) / 2 / 111
  ymin <- ymin - extra_y_deg
  ymax <- ymax + extra_y_deg
}

map_xlim <- c(xmin, xmax)
map_ylim <- c(ymin, ymax)

# ==========================================================================================================
# DEPTH CONTOURS via marmap (NOAA ETOPO bathymetry, cached locally)
# install.packages("marmap") if not yet installed
# ==========================================================================================================
depth_layer   <- list()   # raster fill of sea (deep blue → shallow)
contour_layer <- list()   # depth contour lines + labels
bathy_df      <- NULL

if (requireNamespace("marmap", quietly = TRUE)) {
  # One regional cache covers the whole North Sea / NE Atlantic.
  # Crop to map extent at render time — no per-trip downloads needed.
  BATHY_LON1 <- -8;  BATHY_LON2 <- 10
  BATHY_LAT1 <- 48;  BATHY_LAT2 <- 62
  regional_cache <- file.path(config$cache_path,
    sprintf("bathy_regional_%d_%d_%d_%d.rds",
            BATHY_LON1, BATHY_LON2, BATHY_LAT1, BATHY_LAT2))

  bathy_regional <- tryCatch({
    cached <- if (file.exists(regional_cache)) readRDS(regional_cache) else NULL
    if (!is.null(cached) && inherits(cached, "bathy")) {
      message("  ✓ Regional bathymetry loaded from cache")
      cached
    } else {
      if (file.exists(regional_cache)) file.remove(regional_cache)
      message(glue("  Downloading regional NOAA bathymetry ",
                   "[{BATHY_LON1},{BATHY_LON2}] x [{BATHY_LAT1},{BATHY_LAT2}]",
                   " — one-time download, ~2–5 MB..."))
      b <- marmap::getNOAA.bathy(
        lon1 = BATHY_LON1, lon2 = BATHY_LON2,
        lat1 = BATHY_LAT1, lat2 = BATHY_LAT2,
        resolution = 1, keep = FALSE
      )
      if (inherits(b, "bathy")) {
        saveRDS(b, regional_cache)
        message("  ✓ Regional bathymetry downloaded and cached at: ", regional_cache)
      }
      b
    }
  }, error = function(e) { message("  ⚠ marmap download failed: ", e$message); NULL })

  # Keep full regional bathy — crop to map extent when building each layer
  bathy <- bathy_regional

  if (!is.null(bathy) && inherits(bathy, "bathy")) {
    bathy_df <- tryCatch({
      df <- marmap::fortify.bathy(bathy)
      message(glue("  ✓ bathy_df: {nrow(df)} cells, z [{round(min(df$z))}, {round(max(df$z))}]"))
      df
    }, error = function(e) { message("  ⚠ fortify.bathy failed: ", e$message); NULL })

    if (!is.null(bathy_df)) {

    # Depth fill: crop bathy_df to map extent before plotting
    bathy_crop <- dplyr::filter(bathy_df,
      x >= xmin, x <= xmax, y >= ymin, y <= ymax, z <= 0)
    depth_layer <- list(
      geom_tile(
        data = bathy_crop,
        aes(x = x, y = y, fill = z)
      ),
      scale_fill_gradientn(
        colours  = c("#08306b", "#2171b5", "#6baed6", "#c6dbef"),
        values   = scales::rescale(c(-500, -100, -20, 0)),
        limits   = c(NA, 0),
        na.value = "transparent",
        name     = if (lang == "nl") "Diepte (m)" else "Depth (m)",
        guide    = guide_colorbar(barwidth = 5, barheight = 0.6,
                                  title.position = "top",
                                  direction = "horizontal",
                                  order = 3)
      )
    )

    # Contour lines + labels at ecologically meaningful depths
    if (requireNamespace("metR", quietly = TRUE)) {
      contour_layer <- list(
        metR::geom_contour2(
          data = dplyr::filter(bathy_df, x >= xmin, x <= xmax, y >= ymin, y <= ymax, z < 0),
          aes(x = x, y = y, z = z),
          breaks = c(-20, -50, -100, -200),
          colour = "white", linewidth = 0.2, alpha = 0.5
        ),
        metR::geom_text_contour(
          data = dplyr::filter(bathy_df, x >= xmin, x <= xmax, y >= ymin, y <= ymax, z < 0),
          aes(x = x, y = y, z = z),
          breaks = c(-50, -100, -200),
          size = 2, colour = "white", alpha = 0.8,
          check_overlap = TRUE, skip = 0
        )
      )
    } # end metR contours
    } # end !is.null(bathy_df)
  }
} else {
  message("  ℹ Install 'marmap' for depth: install.packages(c('marmap','metR'))")
}

# ==========================================================================================================
# PLACE NAMES — loaded from flyshoot_places.csv in the project root
# To add/remove places, edit that file — no changes to this Rmd needed.
# ==========================================================================================================
places_file <- p("R", "harmonized", "flyshoot_places.csv")
places <- if (file.exists(places_file)) {
  readr::read_csv(places_file, col_types = "cddc", show_col_types = FALSE)
} else {
  message("  ⚠ flyshoot_places.csv not found at: ", places_file,
          " — using empty places table")
  tibble::tibble(name = character(), lon = numeric(),
                 lat = numeric(), type = character())
}

# Keep unfiltered copy for port coordinate lookups
places_all <- places

# Only show places within the map extent
places <- dplyr::filter(places, lon >= xmin, lon <= xmax,
                                lat >= ymin, lat <= ymax)

places_sf <- if (nrow(places) > 0) {
  sf::st_as_sf(places, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)
} else { NULL }

# ==========================================================================================================
# CROP SPATIAL LAYERS
# ==========================================================================================================
map_bbox <- sf::st_bbox(c(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
                        crs = sf::st_crs(4326))
sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)
world_crop <- sf::st_crop(world_mr_sf, map_bbox)
# Keep only rectangles that contain actual shoot/haul positions (not just bbox overlap)
rect_crop <- if (!is.null(rect_sf)) {
  tryCatch({
    # Collect all shoot positions from available data sources
    pos_lons <- c(
      if (has_haul      && "shoot_lon" %in% names(h))  h$shoot_lon[!is.na(h$shoot_lon)],
      if (has_elog_trek && "shoot_lon" %in% names(et)) et$shoot_lon[!is.na(et$shoot_lon)],
      if (has_elog      && "shoot_lon" %in% names(e))  e$shoot_lon[!is.na(e$shoot_lon)]
    )
    pos_lats <- c(
      if (has_haul      && "shoot_lat" %in% names(h))  h$shoot_lat[!is.na(h$shoot_lat)],
      if (has_elog_trek && "shoot_lat" %in% names(et)) et$shoot_lat[!is.na(et$shoot_lat)],
      if (has_elog      && "shoot_lat" %in% names(e))  e$shoot_lat[!is.na(e$shoot_lat)]
    )
    # If no real positions, fall back to rect centroid positions (elog_rect trips)
    if (length(pos_lons) == 0 && nrow(rect_approx_pos) > 0) {
      pos_lons <- rect_approx_pos$shoot_lon
      pos_lats <- rect_approx_pos$shoot_lat
    }

    if (length(pos_lons) > 0 && length(pos_lats) > 0) {
      pts_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(
        data.frame(lon = pos_lons, lat = pos_lats),
        coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326
      )
      # Keep only rects that contain at least one actual position
      contains <- suppressMessages(
        sf::st_intersects(rect_sf, pts_sf, sparse = TRUE)
      )
      used <- lengths(contains) > 0
      if (any(used)) rect_sf[used, ] else NULL
    } else {
      # Last resort: crop to map bbox
      bbox_poly  <- sf::st_as_sfc(map_bbox)
      intersects <- suppressMessages(sf::st_intersects(rect_sf, bbox_poly, sparse = FALSE)[,1])
      if (any(intersects)) rect_sf[intersects, ] else NULL
    }
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
} else { NULL }
# EEZ boundaries cropped to map extent
eez_crop <- if (!is.null(eez_sf)) {
  tryCatch(sf::st_crop(eez_sf, map_bbox), error = function(e) NULL)
} else { NULL }
sf::sf_use_s2(TRUE)

# ==========================================================================================================
# SHARED BASE MAP LAYERS
# Grid lines removed — ICES rectangles provide the spatial reference grid instead
# ==========================================================================================================

# Degree-minute axis label formatter
fmt_dms <- function(x, is_lon = TRUE) {
  sapply(x, function(v) {
    if (is.na(v)) return("")
    hemi    <- if (is_lon) ifelse(v >= 0, "E", "W") else ifelse(v >= 0, "N", "S")
    v       <- abs(v)
    deg     <- floor(v)
    min_dec <- (v - deg) * 60
    min     <- floor(min_dec)
    if (min == 0) sprintf("%d°%s", deg, hemi)
    else          sprintf("%d°%02d'%s", deg, min, hemi)
  })
}
base_map <- list(
  theme_bw(),
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    panel.border     = element_rect(fill = NA, colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.margin      = unit(c(0, 0, 0, 0), "cm"),
    text             = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.position  = "bottom",
    legend.box       = "horizontal"
  ),
  depth_layer,
  contour_layer,
  # EEZ boundaries — dashed dark line, no fill
  if (!is.null(eez_crop)) geom_sf(data = eez_crop, fill = NA,
      colour = "grey20", linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "solid") else NULL,
  geom_sf(data = world_crop, fill = "grey82", colour = "grey55", linewidth = 0.3),
  coord_sf(xlim = map_xlim, ylim = map_ylim, expand = FALSE),
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = scales::extended_breaks(n = 5)(map_xlim),
    labels = function(x) fmt_dms(x, is_lon = TRUE),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ),
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = scales::extended_breaks(n = 5)(map_ylim),
    labels = function(x) fmt_dms(x, is_lon = FALSE),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ),
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL)
)

# ICES rectangles (lines only — these replace the grid)
# ICES rectangle labels — placed in bottom-left corner of each cell
# Uses st_coordinates on the bbox corner: hjust=0 (left), vjust=1 (bottom)
# Extract SW corner of each ICES rectangle for bottom-left label placement
rect_label_layer <- if (!is.null(rect_crop) && nrow(rect_crop) > 0) {
  # Get SW (bottom-left) corner: min lon, min lat of each rectangle bbox
  sw_corners <- rect_crop %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      bbox   = purrr::map(geometry, sf::st_bbox),
      sw_lon = purrr::map_dbl(bbox, ~.x["xmin"]),
      sw_lat = purrr::map_dbl(bbox, ~.x["ymin"])
    ) %>%
    sf::st_drop_geometry() %>%
    sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("sw_lon", "sw_lat"), crs = 4326)

  list(
    geom_sf(data = rect_crop, fill = NA, colour = "white",
            linewidth = 0.2, alpha = 0.9, linetype = "solid"),
    # Shadow for readability (slightly offset dark text underneath)
    geom_sf_text(data = sw_corners, aes(label = rect),
                 colour   = "grey10",
                 size     = 2.8,
                 hjust    = 0, vjust = 0,
                 nudge_x  = (xmax - xmin) * 0.005,
                 nudge_y  = (ymax - ymin) * 0.005,
                 fontface = "bold"),
    # White text on top
    geom_sf_text(data = sw_corners, aes(label = rect),
                 colour   = "white",
                 size     = 2.8,
                 hjust    = 0, vjust = 0,
                 nudge_x  = (xmax - xmin) * 0.004,
                 nudge_y  = (ymax - ymin) * 0.004,
                 fontface = "bold")
  )
} else { list() }

# Place name layer (ports = filled triangle, cities = open circle)
places_layer <- if (!is.null(places_sf)) {
  ports <- dplyr::filter(places_sf, type == "port")
  cities <- dplyr::filter(places_sf, type == "city")
  layers <- list()
  if (nrow(cities) > 0) {
    layers <- c(layers, list(
      geom_sf(data = cities, shape = 21, size = 1.5,
              fill = "white", colour = "grey30", stroke = 0.5),
      geom_sf_text(data = cities, aes(label = name),
                   size = 2.2, colour = "grey20",
                   nudge_y = (ymax - ymin) * 0.018,
                   check_overlap = TRUE)
    ))
  }
  if (nrow(ports) > 0) {
    layers <- c(layers, list(
      geom_sf(data = ports, shape = 24, size = 2,
              fill = "orange", colour = "darkorange3", stroke = 0.5),
      geom_sf_text(data = ports, aes(label = name),
                   size = 2.4, colour = "darkorange4", fontface = "bold",
                   nudge_y = (ymax - ymin) * 0.018,
                   check_overlap = TRUE)
    ))
  }
  layers
} else { list() }

# ==========================================================================================================
# DOCUMENT COLOUR PALETTE — use these throughout for consistency
# ==========================================================================================================
pal_aanvoer   <- "#5B8DB8"   # steel blue  — landings / aanvoer
pal_discards  <- "#D96B4F"   # terracotta  — discards
pal_header    <- "#D5E8F0"   # light blue  — table headers (keep existing)
pal_cur_year  <- "#C0392B"   # deep red    — current year line (fig 5)
pal_prev_year <- "#2E6DA4"   # mid blue    — previous year line (fig 5)
pal_hist_year <- "grey65"    # grey        — older years (fig 5)
pal_day_pal   <- function(n) scales::hue_pal(h = c(200, 340), l = 60, c = 70)(n)
# ^ day colours: cool blue→warm pink, avoids clash with species colours

# ==========================================================================================================
# SPECIES COLOUR PALETTE — fixed colours per FAO 3-alpha code
# Consistent across all plots in the report. Extend as needed.
# ==========================================================================================================
species_palette <- c(
  SQR="#7B2D8B", SQC="#7B2D8B",  # squid — purple
  MAC="#1F6FB2",                   # mackerel — dark blue
  HOM="#4AA8D8",                   # horse mackerel — mid blue
  MUR="#E84646",                   # red mullet — red
  WHG="#F4A742",                   # whiting — orange
  CTC="#E8C84A",                   # cuttlefish — yellow
  BSS="#3FAD6B",                   # sea bass — green
  RJC="#8B4513",                   # ray — brown
  COD="#2E8B57",                   # cod — sea green
  POK="#20B2AA",                   # saithe — teal
  HAD="#9370DB",                   # haddock — medium purple
  PLE="#FF7F50",                   # plaice — coral
  SOL="#FFD700",                   # sole — gold
  TUR="#4682B4",                   # turbot — steel blue
  BLL="#87CEEB",                   # brill — sky blue
  DAB="#DEB887",                   # dab — burlywood
  LEM="#F0E68C",                   # lemon sole — khaki
  GUU="#98FB98",                   # gurnard — pale green
  GUR="#90EE90",                   # gurnard spp — light green
  BRB="#FF69B4",                   # red bream — hot pink
  RJI="#A0522D",                   # blonde ray — sienna
  PIL="#6495ED",                   # pilchard — cornflower
  SPR="#48D1CC",                   # sprat — medium turquoise
  HER="#191970",                   # herring — midnight blue
  OCC="#FF8C00",                   # octopus — dark orange
  ANK="#DC143C",                   # monkfish — crimson
  SCR="#556B2F",                   # scad — dark olive
  Other="grey65"                   # catch-all
)

# Function: get colour for a species code, falling back to a generated colour
sp_colour <- function(codes) {
  cols <- species_palette[codes]
  missing <- is.na(cols)
  if (any(missing)) {
    # generate reproducible colours for unknown codes
    set.seed(42)
    extra <- scales::hue_pal()(sum(missing))
    cols[missing] <- extra
  }
  setNames(as.character(cols), codes)
}

# ==========================================================================================================
# FIGURE HEIGHT CAP — adjust this single value to control max plot height on page.
# A4 text area ~9.2". Reserve ~0.3" for caption. word_scale = 6.3/10 = 0.63.
# max_fig_h = (9.2 - 0.3) / 0.63 ≈ 14.1 knitr device inches.
# ==========================================================================================================
max_fig_h <- 14.6   # ← full A4 page height in knitr inches (9.2" / 0.63)

# Date range for titles
report_start <- if (has_elog) min(e$date, na.rm = TRUE) else
                if (has_haul) min(h$date, na.rm = TRUE) else startdate
report_end   <- if (has_elog) max(e$date, na.rm = TRUE) else
                if (has_haul) max(h$date, na.rm = TRUE) else enddate

```


# `r paste(setvessel, collapse = ", ")` — `r txt("hdr_report")`

### `r txt("plot_trip_lbl")` `r paste(settrip, collapse=", ")` | `r format(report_start, "%d %b")` – `r format(report_end, "%d %b %Y")`

`r txt("hdr_generated")`: `r format(Sys.time(), '%d/%m/%Y')`

&nbsp;

<!-- ########################################################################################## -->
<!-- Table 1: Trip summary                                                                     -->
<!-- ########################################################################################## -->

```{r tab_samenvatting}

tab_nums("samenvatting", display = FALSE, caption = txt("cap_samenvatting"))

# ── Trip metrics ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Spatial columns (fao_division, ices_rect) are added by add_spatial_attributes()
# to the haul dataset. For elog_trek and elog these columns may be absent or all-NA
# (pefa_trek files don't carry position-derived spatial data at the catch row level).
# Strategy: derive date/haul counts from best available source, then overlay spatial
# columns from haul data if present.

get_spatial_from_haul <- function() {
  if (!has_haul) return(NULL)
  h %>%
    dplyr::group_by(vessel, trip_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      divisions = if ("fao_division" %in% names(.))
                    paste(sort(unique(na.omit(fao_division))), collapse = "; ")
                  else NA_character_,
      rects     = if ("ices_rect" %in% names(.))
                    paste(sort(unique(na.omit(ices_rect))), collapse = "; ")
                  else NA_character_,
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

if (has_elog_trek) {
  c1 <- et %>%
    dplyr::group_by(vessel, trip_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      nhauls    = dplyr::n_distinct(haul_id),
      startdate = min(date, na.rm = TRUE),
      enddate   = max(date, na.rm = TRUE),
      weeks     = paste(sort(unique(isoweek(date))), collapse = ", "),
      # Use elog_trek spatial if populated, else will be overridden from haul below
      divisions = paste(sort(unique(na.omit(fao_division))), collapse = "; "),
      rects     = paste(sort(unique(na.omit(ices_rect))), collapse = "; "),
      .groups   = "drop"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      ndays       = as.numeric(difftime(enddate, startdate, units = "days")) + 1,
      hauls_p_day = round(nhauls / ndays, 1)
    )
} else if (has_elog) {
  c1 <- e %>%
    dplyr::group_by(vessel, trip_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      startdate = min(date, na.rm = TRUE),
      enddate   = max(date, na.rm = TRUE),
      weeks     = paste(sort(unique(isoweek(date))), collapse = ", "),
      divisions = paste(sort(unique(na.omit(fao_division))), collapse = "; "),
      rects     = paste(sort(unique(na.omit(ices_rect))), collapse = "; "),
      .groups   = "drop"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(ndays = as.numeric(difftime(enddate, startdate, units = "days")) + 1)
} else {
  c1 <- h %>%
    dplyr::group_by(vessel, trip_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      nhauls    = dplyr::n_distinct(haul_id),
      startdate = min(date, na.rm = TRUE),
      enddate   = max(date, na.rm = TRUE),
      weeks     = paste(sort(unique(isoweek(date))), collapse = ", "),
      divisions = if ("fao_division" %in% names(.))
                    paste(sort(unique(na.omit(fao_division))), collapse = "; ") else NA_character_,
      rects     = if ("ices_rect" %in% names(.))
                    paste(sort(unique(na.omit(ices_rect))),    collapse = "; ") else NA_character_,
      .groups   = "drop"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      ndays       = as.numeric(difftime(enddate, startdate, units = "days")) + 1,
      hauls_p_day = round(nhauls / ndays, 1)
    )
}

# Override divisions/rects from haul when the primary source has empty values
# (haul has add_spatial_attributes() applied; elog_trek/elog rows often do not)
spatial_from_haul <- get_spatial_from_haul()
if (!is.null(spatial_from_haul)) {
  c1 <- c1 %>%
    dplyr::left_join(spatial_from_haul, by = c("vessel", "trip_id"),
                     suffix = c("", "_haul")) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      divisions = dplyr::if_else(
        is.na(divisions) | divisions == "",
        divisions_haul, divisions),
      rects = dplyr::if_else(
        is.na(rects) | rects == "",
        rects_haul, rects)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::select(-dplyr::ends_with("_haul"))
}

# ── Total distance from vessel_movement ──────────────────────────────────────
if (has_vm) {
  # Compute trip distance from vm positions using haversine formula
  # Falls back to stored 'distance' column if present and non-NA
  haversine_nm <- function(lon1, lat1, lon2, lat2) {
    R   <- 6371
    phi <- (lat2 - lat1) * pi / 180
    lam <- (lon2 - lon1) * pi / 180
    a   <- sin(phi/2)^2 + cos(lat1*pi/180)*cos(lat2*pi/180)*sin(lam/2)^2
    2 * R * asin(pmin(1, sqrt(a))) / 1.852  # km -> nm
  }

  dist_summary <- if ("distance" %in% names(vm) &&
                       sum(!is.na(vm$distance)) > 0) {
    # Use stored distance column (check units: should be nm)
    vm %>%
      dplyr::group_by(trip_id) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(total_distance = round(sum(distance, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
                       .groups = "drop")
  } else {
    # Compute from lat/lon
    vm %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(lat), !is.na(lon)) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(vessel, trip_id, date) %>%
      dplyr::group_by(trip_id) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        seg_nm = haversine_nm(
          dplyr::lag(lon, default = dplyr::first(lon)),
          dplyr::lag(lat, default = dplyr::first(lat)),
          lon, lat)
      ) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(total_distance = round(sum(seg_nm, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
                       .groups = "drop")
  }
  c1 <- dplyr::left_join(c1, dist_summary, by = "trip_id")
}

# ── Total distance from vessel_movement ──────────────────────────────────────


# ── Gear settings ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# mesh_size_mm: always prefer elog (reliably populated from e-logbook system).
# Other gear columns (vertical_opening_m, cable_length_m, etc.): from haul
# (treklijst source), falling back to elog when haul is absent.
gear_cols <- c("gear_type", "mesh_size_mm", "vertical_opening_m",
               "cable_length_m", "cable_thickness_mm",
               "groundrope_length_m", "escape_panel")

gear_src <- if (has_haul && any(gear_cols %in% names(h))) { h
} else if (has_elog && any(c("gear_type","mesh_size_mm") %in% names(e))) { e
} else { NULL }

if (!is.null(gear_src)) {
  gear_info <- gear_src %>%
    dplyr::select(trip_id, dplyr::any_of(gear_cols)) %>%
    dplyr::distinct() %>%
    dplyr::group_by(trip_id) %>%
    dplyr::slice(1) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    # Coerce mesh_size_mm to character so it can later hold "80; 100" style strings
    dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(dplyr::any_of("mesh_size_mm"), as.character))
  c1 <- dplyr::left_join(c1, gear_info, by = "trip_id")
}

# Override mesh_size_mm from elog — always use elog as the authoritative source.
# Collapse all distinct mesh sizes into a single string (e.g. "80; 100").
# This completely replaces whatever gear_info put into c1 for mesh_size_mm.
if (has_elog && "mesh_size_mm" %in% names(e)) {
  mesh_from_elog <- e %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(mesh_size_mm)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(trip_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      mesh_size_mm_elog = paste(sort(unique(as.integer(mesh_size_mm))), collapse = "; "),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # Remove existing mesh_size_mm from c1 entirely before joining, so there is
  # no coalesce ambiguity — elog value always wins unconditionally.
  c1 <- c1 %>%
    dplyr::select(-dplyr::any_of("mesh_size_mm")) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(mesh_from_elog %>% dplyr::rename(mesh_size_mm = mesh_size_mm_elog),
                     by = "trip_id")
}

# ── Build display table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# Helper to apply consistent flextable styling
style_ft <- function(ft, caption = NULL) {
  ft <- flextable::bold(ft, part = "header")
  ft <- flextable::bg(ft, part = "header", bg = "#D5E8F0")
  ft <- flextable::hline_top(ft, part = "header",
           border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 1.5))
  ft <- flextable::hline_bottom(ft, part = "header",
           border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 1.5))
  ft <- flextable::hline_bottom(ft, part = "body",
           border = officer::fp_border(color = "#AAAAAA", width = 0.5))
  ft <- flextable::autofit(ft)
  # Prevent Word from repeating header row at bottom of table
  ft <- flextable::set_table_properties(
    ft, opts_word = list(split = FALSE, keep_with_next = TRUE)
  )
  if (!is.null(caption)) ft <- flextable::set_caption(ft, caption)
  ft
}
# ── Part A: trip logistics ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
part_a <- c1 %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    startdate = format(startdate, "%d/%m/%Y"),
    enddate   = format(enddate,   "%d/%m/%Y"),
    # total_distance from trip parquet (nm); round to 1 decimal
    total_distance = if ("total_distance" %in% names(.))
      round(total_distance, 1) else NA_real_
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(trip_id, startdate, enddate, ndays,
                dplyr::any_of(c("nhauls", "hauls_p_day")),
                weeks, divisions, rects,
                dplyr::any_of("total_distance")) %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    !!txt("col_trip")      := trip_id,
    !!txt("col_from")      := startdate,
    !!txt("col_to")        := enddate,
    !!txt("col_days")      := ndays,
    !!txt("col_weeks")     := weeks,
    !!txt("col_divisions") := divisions,
    !!txt("col_rects")     := rects
  ) %>%
  {
    d <- .
    if ("nhauls"          %in% names(d)) d <- dplyr::rename(d, !!txt("col_hauls")     := nhauls)
    if ("hauls_p_day"     %in% names(d)) d <- dplyr::rename(d, !!txt("col_hauls_day") := hauls_p_day)
    if ("total_distance"  %in% names(d)) d <- dplyr::rename(d, !!txt("col_distance")  := total_distance)
    d
  }

ft_a <- flextable::flextable(part_a)
ft_a <- style_ft(ft_a, caption = tab_nums("samenvatting"))
# Cap width of Divisie and ICES rect columns so long lists wrap rather than
# pushing the Distance column off the page.
for (col_nm in intersect(c(txt("col_divisions"), txt("col_rects")), names(part_a))) {
  ft_a <- flextable::width(ft_a, j = col_nm, width = 1.1)
}
flextable::set_table_properties(ft_a, width = 1, layout = "autofit")

# ── Part B: gear settings ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
gear_cols_b <- c("gear_type", "mesh_size_mm", "vertical_opening_m",
                 "cable_length_m", "cable_thickness_mm")

if (any(gear_cols_b %in% names(c1))) {
  gear_rename <- c(
    "gear_type"         = txt("col_gear"),
    "mesh_size_mm"      = txt("col_mesh"),
    "vertical_opening_m"= txt("col_vopening"),
    "cable_length_m"    = txt("col_cable"),
    "cable_thickness_mm"= txt("col_cablethick")
  )
  present_gear <- intersect(names(gear_rename), names(c1))

  part_b <- c1 %>%
    dplyr::select(trip_id, dplyr::any_of(names(gear_rename))) %>%
    dplyr::rename(!!txt("col_trip") := trip_id,
                  dplyr::any_of(setNames(present_gear, gear_rename[present_gear])))

  style_ft(flextable::flextable(part_b))
}
```


&nbsp;

<!-- ########################################################################################## -->
<!-- Table 2: Catch by species                                                                 -->
<!-- ########################################################################################## -->

```{r tab_vangst_soort}

src_lbl <- dplyr::case_when(
  has_elog_trek && nrow(et) > 0 && "weight_kg" %in% names(et) ~ "elog_trek",
  has_kisten    && nrow(m)  > 0                                ~ "kisten",
  has_elog      && nrow(e)  > 0                                ~ "elog",
  TRUE                                                         ~ "?"
)
tab_nums("catchbyspecies", display = FALSE,
         caption = paste0(txt("cap_catch"), " [", src_lbl, "]"))

# Data source priority for catch by species: elog_trek > kisten > elog
catch_src <- if (has_elog_trek && nrow(et) > 0 && "weight_kg" %in% names(et)) {
  et
} else if (has_kisten && nrow(m) > 0) {
  m
} else if (has_elog && nrow(e) > 0) {
  e
} else { tibble::tibble() }

if (nrow(catch_src) > 0) {

  # Ordered species (by total weight descending)
  species_order <- catch_src %>%
    dplyr::group_by(species_code) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(total = sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(total)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(dplyr::select(asfis, species, dutchname, englishname),
                     by = c("species_code" = "species"))

  # Ordered dates for column headers
  trip_dates <- sort(unique(catch_src$date[!is.na(catch_src$date)]))
  date_labels <- format(trip_dates, "%d/%m")  # e.g. "01/12"

  # Daily catch per species (wide pivot)
  daily_wide <- catch_src %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(date)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(date_lbl = format(date, "%d/%m")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(species_code, date_lbl) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(kg = round(sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop") %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = date_lbl, values_from = kg,
                       values_fill = 0L) %>%
    # Ensure date columns appear in chronological order
    dplyr::select(species_code, dplyr::any_of(date_labels))

  # Total per species
  totals <- catch_src %>%
    dplyr::group_by(species_code) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(!!txt("col_total_kg") := round(sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop")

  # Assemble: keep species_code for joining, rename at the very end
  tbl <- species_order %>%
    dplyr::select(species_code,
                  !!txt("col_species") := !!species_name_col) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(daily_wide, by = "species_code") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(totals,     by = "species_code") %>%
    # Now rename species_code to the translated Code column and reorder
    dplyr::rename(!!txt("col_code") := species_code) %>%
    dplyr::select(!!txt("col_species"), !!txt("col_code"), dplyr::everything())

  # Columns to sum and right-align (everything except species name and code)
  species_col <- txt("col_species")
  code_col    <- txt("col_code")
  num_cols    <- setdiff(names(tbl), c(species_col, code_col))

  # Grand total row
  totaal_row <- tbl %>%
    dplyr::summarise(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(num_cols), sum, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      !!txt("col_species") := txt("total_row"),
      !!txt("col_code")    := "",
      .before = 1
    )

  tbl <- dplyr::bind_rows(tbl, totaal_row)
  n_data <- nrow(tbl) - 1

  # Build flextable
  ft <- flextable::flextable(tbl)
  ft <- flextable::set_caption(ft, tab_nums("catchbyspecies"))
  ft <- flextable::bold(ft, part = "header")
  ft <- flextable::bold(ft, i = nrow(tbl), part = "body")
  ft <- flextable::bg(ft, part = "header", bg = "#D5E8F0")
  ft <- flextable::bg(ft, i = nrow(tbl), bg = "#F0F0F0", part = "body")
  ft <- flextable::hline_top(ft, part = "header",
         border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 1.5))
  ft <- flextable::hline_bottom(ft, part = "header",
         border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 1.5))
  ft <- flextable::hline(ft, i = n_data, part = "body",
         border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 1.0))
  ft <- flextable::hline_bottom(ft, part = "body",
         border = officer::fp_border(color = "#AAAAAA", width = 0.5))
  ft <- flextable::colformat_num(ft, j = num_cols, big.mark = ",")
  ft <- flextable::align(ft, j = num_cols, align = "right", part = "all")
  ft <- flextable::align(ft, j = c(species_col, code_col), align = "left", part = "all")
  ft <- flextable::autofit(ft)
  ft
}
```


&nbsp;

<!-- ########################################################################################## -->
<!-- Table 3: Catch by species per economic zone                                               -->
<!-- ########################################################################################## -->

```{r tab_vangst_zone}

tab_nums("catchbyzone", display = FALSE, caption = txt("cap_catch_zone"))

# Use elog as source — it reliably carries economic_zone from the e-logbook system.
# Fall back to elog_trek if elog is absent and elog_trek has economic_zone.
zone_src <- if (has_elog && nrow(e) > 0 && "economic_zone" %in% names(e)) {
  e
} else if (has_elog_trek && nrow(et) > 0 && "economic_zone" %in% names(et)) {
  et
} else { tibble::tibble() }

if (nrow(zone_src) > 0 && any(!is.na(zone_src$economic_zone))) {

  # Same species ordering as Table 2 (by total weight descending)
  zone_species_order <- zone_src %>%
    dplyr::group_by(species_code) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(total = sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(desc(total)) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(dplyr::select(asfis, species, dutchname, englishname),
                     by = c("species_code" = "species"))

  # Ordered zones for column headers (alphabetical; NA last)
  zone_levels <- sort(unique(na.omit(zone_src$economic_zone)))

  # Catch per species per zone (wide pivot)
  zone_wide <- zone_src %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(economic_zone)) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(species_code, economic_zone) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(kg = round(sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop") %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = economic_zone, values_from = kg,
                       values_fill = 0L) %>%
    dplyr::select(species_code, dplyr::any_of(zone_levels))

  # Total per species (across all zones)
  zone_totals <- zone_src %>%
    dplyr::group_by(species_code) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(!!txt("col_total_kg") := round(sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE)),
                     .groups = "drop")

  # Assemble table
  tbl_z <- zone_species_order %>%
    dplyr::select(species_code,
                  !!txt("col_species") := !!species_name_col) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(zone_wide,   by = "species_code") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(zone_totals, by = "species_code") %>%
    dplyr::rename(!!txt("col_code") := species_code) %>%
    dplyr::select(!!txt("col_species"), !!txt("col_code"), dplyr::everything())

  species_col_z <- txt("col_species")
  code_col_z    <- txt("col_code")
  num_cols_z    <- setdiff(names(tbl_z), c(species_col_z, code_col_z))

  # Grand total row
  totaal_row_z <- tbl_z %>%
    dplyr::summarise(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(num_cols_z), sum, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      !!txt("col_species") := txt("total_row"),
      !!txt("col_code")    := "",
      .before = 1
    )

  tbl_z  <- dplyr::bind_rows(tbl_z, totaal_row_z)
  n_data_z <- nrow(tbl_z) - 1

  ft_z <- flextable::flextable(tbl_z)
  ft_z <- flextable::set_caption(ft_z, tab_nums("catchbyzone"))
  ft_z <- flextable::bold(ft_z, part = "header")
  ft_z <- flextable::bold(ft_z, i = nrow(tbl_z), part = "body")
  ft_z <- flextable::bg(ft_z, part = "header", bg = "#D5E8F0")
  ft_z <- flextable::bg(ft_z, i = nrow(tbl_z), bg = "#F0F0F0", part = "body")
  ft_z <- flextable::hline_top(ft_z, part = "header",
           border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 1.5))
  ft_z <- flextable::hline_bottom(ft_z, part = "header",
           border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 1.5))
  ft_z <- flextable::hline(ft_z, i = n_data_z, part = "body",
           border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 1.0))
  ft_z <- flextable::hline_bottom(ft_z, part = "body",
           border = officer::fp_border(color = "#AAAAAA", width = 0.5))
  ft_z <- flextable::colformat_num(ft_z, j = num_cols_z, big.mark = ",")
  ft_z <- flextable::align(ft_z, j = num_cols_z, align = "right", part = "all")
  ft_z <- flextable::align(ft_z, j = c(species_col_z, code_col_z), align = "left", part = "all")
  ft_z <- flextable::autofit(ft_z)
  ft_z
}
```


&nbsp;

<!-- ########################################################################################## -->
<!-- Table 4: Catch overview — flat table by source / presentation / size / discard reason    -->
<!-- ########################################################################################## -->

```{r tab_catch_overview}

tab_nums("catchoverview", display = FALSE, caption = txt("cap_catch_overview"))

# Build one row per source × presentation × size_category × discard_reason combination.
# Only include rows that actually have data; empty slots are silently dropped.
# Columns present depend on what each source provides — no NA-only columns shown.

overview_rows <- list()

# ── Elog ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if (has_elog && nrow(e) > 0) {
  grp_cols <- intersect(c("presentation", "size_category", "discard_reason"), names(e))
  overview_rows[["elog"]] <- e %>%
    dplyr::mutate(source_lbl = txt("row_elog")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(source_lbl, dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(grp_cols))) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(weight_kg = round(sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop")
}

# ── Elog trek ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if (has_elog_trek && nrow(et) > 0) {
  grp_cols_et <- intersect(c("presentation", "size_category", "discard_reason"), names(et))
  overview_rows[["elog_trek"]] <- et %>%
    dplyr::mutate(source_lbl = txt("row_elog_trek")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(source_lbl, dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(grp_cols_et))) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(weight_kg = round(sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop")
}

# ── Kisten ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
if (has_kisten && nrow(m) > 0 && !has_elog_trek) {
  grp_cols_m <- intersect(c("presentation", "size_category", "discard_reason"), names(m))
  overview_rows[["kisten"]] <- m %>%
    dplyr::mutate(source_lbl = txt("row_kisten")) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(source_lbl, dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(grp_cols_m))) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(weight_kg = round(sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop")
}

if (length(overview_rows) > 0) {

  ov <- dplyr::bind_rows(overview_rows)

  # Drop columns that are entirely NA (source doesn't provide them)
  ov <- ov %>% dplyr::select(dplyr::where(~ !all(is.na(.))))

  # Drop rows where weight is 0 or NA
  ov <- ov %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(weight_kg), weight_kg > 0)

  # Rename columns to translated labels.
  # rename() needs c(new_name = "old_name") — build via setNames.
  col_old <- c("source_lbl", "presentation", "size_category",
               "discard_reason", "weight_kg")
  col_new <- c(txt("col_source"), txt("col_presentation"),
               if (lang == "nl") "Grootte" else "Size",
               txt("col_discard_rsn"), txt("col_weight_total"))
  keep       <- col_old %in% names(ov)
  rename_vec <- setNames(col_old[keep], col_new[keep])
  ov <- dplyr::rename(ov, dplyr::all_of(rename_vec))

  kg_col <- txt("col_weight_total")

  src_col    <- txt("col_source")
  sub_suffix <- if (lang == "nl") " subtotaal" else " subtotal"

  # Build subtotal rows: one per source, labelled with suffix
  subtotals <- ov %>%
    dplyr::group_by(.data[[src_col]]) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(!!kg_col := sum(.data[[kg_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
                     .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(!!src_col := paste0(.data[[src_col]], sub_suffix))

  # Interleave data rows and subtotal rows using a sort key:
  # give each data row a sequence number within its group, give each
  # subtotal row a sequence number just after the last data row.
  ov_seq <- ov %>%
    dplyr::group_by(.data[[src_col]]) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(.seq = dplyr::row_number(),
                  .is_sub = FALSE) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()

  sub_seq <- ov %>%
    dplyr::group_by(.data[[src_col]]) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(.seq = dplyr::n() + 0.5,    # sorts after last data row
                     !!kg_col := sum(.data[[kg_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
                     .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(!!src_col := paste0(.data[[src_col]], sub_suffix),
                  .is_sub = TRUE)

  ov_final <- dplyr::bind_rows(ov_seq, sub_seq) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(.data[[src_col]], .seq) %>%
    dplyr::select(-.seq, -.is_sub)
  # no grand total — elog and elog_trek measure the same catch
  n_rows   <- nrow(ov_final)

  # Row indices for styling
  subtotal_rows <- which(grepl(
    if (lang == "nl") " subtotaal" else " subtotal",
    ov_final[[src_col]], fixed = TRUE))

  ft_ov <- flextable::flextable(ov_final)
  ft_ov <- flextable::set_caption(ft_ov, tab_nums("catchoverview"))
  ft_ov <- flextable::bold(ft_ov, part = "header")
  ft_ov <- flextable::bold(ft_ov, i = subtotal_rows, part = "body")
  ft_ov <- flextable::italic(ft_ov, i = subtotal_rows, part = "body")
  ft_ov <- flextable::bg(ft_ov, part = "header", bg = "#D5E8F0")
  ft_ov <- flextable::bg(ft_ov, i = subtotal_rows, bg = "#EAF2FB", part = "body")
  ft_ov <- flextable::hline_top(ft_ov, part = "header",
             border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 1.5))
  ft_ov <- flextable::hline_bottom(ft_ov, part = "header",
             border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 1.5))
  for (sr in subtotal_rows) {
    ft_ov <- flextable::hline(ft_ov, i = sr, part = "body",
               border = officer::fp_border(color = "#2E75B6", width = 0.75))
  }
  ft_ov <- flextable::hline_bottom(ft_ov, part = "body",
             border = officer::fp_border(color = "#AAAAAA", width = 0.5))
  ft_ov <- flextable::align(ft_ov, j = kg_col, align = "right", part = "all")
  ft_ov <- flextable::colformat_num(ft_ov, j = kg_col, big.mark = ",")
  ft_ov <- flextable::autofit(ft_ov)
  ft_ov
}
```


\newpage

<!-- ########################################################################################## -->
<!-- Figure 1: Map of fishing locations                                                        -->
<!-- ########################################################################################## -->

```{r fig_tripmap_setup}
# Compute aspect-corrected figure height from map extent
lat_correction <- cos(mean(c(ymin, ymax)) * pi / 180)
data_aspect    <- ((ymax - ymin) / (xmax - xmin)) / lat_correction
fig_h_tripmap  <- min(10 * data_aspect + 1.5, max_fig_h)
```

```{r fig_tripmap, fig.height=fig_h_tripmap}

fig_nums("tripmap", display = FALSE, caption = txt("cap_tripmap"))



if (!is.null(pos_sf)) {

  # ── Catch weight per haul ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Prefer elog_trek (haul-level catch); fall back to elog aggregated to haul
  haul_catch_source <- "none"
  haul_catch <- if (has_elog_trek && any(!is.na(et$shoot_lon))) {
    haul_catch_source <- "elog_trek"
    et %>%
      dplyr::group_by(haul_id, shoot_lon, shoot_lat) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(
        weight_kg = sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(shoot_lon), !is.na(shoot_lat))
  } else if (has_haul && any(!is.na(h$shoot_lon))) {
    haul_catch_source <- "haul"
    # Use total_catch_kg if available, otherwise sum from elog/kisten
    h_base <- h %>%
      dplyr::select(haul_id, shoot_lon, shoot_lat,
                    dplyr::any_of("total_catch_kg")) %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(shoot_lon), !is.na(shoot_lat))
    if ("total_catch_kg" %in% names(h_base)) {
      dplyr::rename(h_base, weight_kg = total_catch_kg)
    } else if (has_elog_trek) {
      # join weights from elog_trek
      wts <- et %>% dplyr::group_by(haul_id) %>%
        dplyr::summarise(weight_kg = sum(weight_kg, na.rm=TRUE), .groups="drop")
      dplyr::left_join(h_base, wts, by="haul_id") %>%
        dplyr::mutate(weight_kg = dplyr::if_else(is.na(weight_kg), 0, weight_kg))
    } else if (has_kisten) {
      wts <- m %>% dplyr::group_by(haul_id) %>%
        dplyr::summarise(weight_kg = sum(weight_kg, na.rm=TRUE), .groups="drop")
      dplyr::left_join(h_base, wts, by="haul_id") %>%
        dplyr::mutate(weight_kg = dplyr::if_else(is.na(weight_kg), 0, weight_kg))
    } else {
      dplyr::mutate(h_base, weight_kg = 0)
    }
  } else if (has_elog && any(!is.na(e$shoot_lon))) {
    haul_catch_source <- "elog"
    grp_cols <- intersect(c("haul_id", "shoot_lon", "shoot_lat"), names(e))
    e %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(shoot_lon), !is.na(shoot_lat)) %>%
      dplyr::group_by(dplyr::across(dplyr::all_of(grp_cols))) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(weight_kg = sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
      { if (!"haul_id" %in% names(.)) dplyr::mutate(., haul_id = dplyr::row_number()) else . }
  } else if (has_elog && "ices_rect" %in% names(e) && any(!is.na(e$ices_rect))) {
    # Fallback: no actual positions — use ICES rect centroids with daily catch totals
    haul_catch_source <- "elog_rect"
    rect_coords <- ices_rect_to_lonlat(e$ices_rect)
    e %>%
      dplyr::mutate(shoot_lon = rect_coords$lon, shoot_lat = rect_coords$lat) %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(shoot_lon), !is.na(shoot_lat)) %>%
      dplyr::group_by(date, shoot_lon, shoot_lat) %>%
      dplyr::summarise(weight_kg = sum(weight_kg, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(haul_id = dplyr::row_number())
  } else {
    tibble::tibble()
  }

  haul_sf <- if (nrow(haul_catch) > 0) {
    sf::st_as_sf(haul_catch, coords = c("shoot_lon", "shoot_lat"),
                 crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)
  } else { NULL }

  # ── Route line from vessel_movement ──────────────────────────────────────────
  # Port coordinates are pre-filled in the vm parquet at processing time.
  route_sf <- if (has_vm) {
    vm_trip <- vm %>%
      dplyr::filter(vessel %in% setvessel, trip_id %in% settrip,
                    !is.na(lat), !is.na(lon)) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(vessel, trip_id, date)
    if (nrow(vm_trip) > 1) {
      lines <- vm_trip %>%
        dplyr::group_by(vessel, trip_id) %>%
        dplyr::group_map(~ sf::st_linestring(as.matrix(.x[, c("lon","lat")]))) %>%
        sf::st_sfc(crs = 4326)
      trip_keys <- vm_trip %>% dplyr::distinct(vessel, trip_id)
      sf::st_sf(trip_keys, geometry = lines)
    } else { NULL }
  } else if (has_haul && nrow(h) > 0 &&
             sum(!is.na(h$shoot_lon) & !is.na(h$shoot_lat)) > 1) {
    # Fallback: connect haul shoot positions in chronological order
    haul_pts <- h %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(shoot_lon), !is.na(shoot_lat)) %>%
      dplyr::arrange(trip_id, date, haul_id) %>%
      dplyr::group_by(vessel, trip_id)
    lines <- haul_pts %>%
      dplyr::group_map(~ sf::st_linestring(as.matrix(.x[, c("shoot_lon","shoot_lat")]))) %>%
      sf::st_sfc(crs = 4326)
    trip_keys <- dplyr::distinct(haul_pts, vessel, trip_id)
    sf::st_sf(dplyr::ungroup(trip_keys), geometry = lines)
  } else { NULL }
  trip_colour_map <- NULL  # built after day_colours below

  # ── Port locations — from vessel_movement lat/lon, or looked up from places ───
  # Port locations: from vessel_movement departure/arrival events.
  # vm already has ports derived from haul departure_port/arrival_port columns.
  # If vm is missing, fall back to haul columns directly.
  ports_raw <- if (has_vm) {
    vm %>%
      dplyr::filter(vessel %in% setvessel, trip_id %in% settrip,
                    event_type %in% c("departure", "arrival")) %>%
      dplyr::distinct(vessel, trip_id, event_type, port, lat, lon)
  } else if (has_haul && nrow(h) > 0) {
    dplyr::bind_rows(
      h %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(departure_port)) %>%
        dplyr::slice(1) %>%
        dplyr::transmute(vessel, trip_id, event_type = "departure",
                         port = departure_port, lat = NA_real_, lon = NA_real_),
      h %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(arrival_port)) %>%
        dplyr::slice(dplyr::n()) %>%
        dplyr::transmute(vessel, trip_id, event_type = "arrival",
                         port = arrival_port, lat = NA_real_, lon = NA_real_)
    ) %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(port))
  } else { NULL }

  # Port coordinates are pre-filled in vm parquet — just convert to sf
  ports_sf <- if (!is.null(ports_raw) && nrow(ports_raw) > 0) {
    ports_located <- dplyr::filter(ports_raw, !is.na(lat), !is.na(lon))
    if (nrow(ports_located) > 0)
      sf::st_as_sf(ports_located, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)
    else NULL
  } else { NULL }

  # ── Build plot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  p <- ggplot() +
    base_map +
    rect_label_layer

  # Route line — one per trip, coloured by that trip's day colour
  if (!is.null(route_sf)) {
    route_plot <- if (!is.null(trip_colour_map)) {
      dplyr::left_join(route_sf, trip_colour_map, by = "trip_id") %>%
        dplyr::mutate(colour = dplyr::if_else(is.na(colour), "yellow", colour))
    } else {
      dplyr::mutate(route_sf, colour = "yellow")
    }
    p <- p + geom_sf(data = route_plot, aes(colour = colour),
                       linewidth = 1.2, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.95,
                       show.legend = FALSE) +
             scale_colour_identity()
  }

  # Reset fill scale so bubbles can use a discrete fill
  # independently of the continuous depth fill
  p <- p + ggnewscale::new_scale_fill()

  # Haul bubbles: SIZE = catch weight, COLOUR = fishing day (discrete)
  if (!is.null(haul_sf)) {
    # Add day label for discrete colour
    # Join one date per haul_id (slice_max avoids duplicates when multiple rows/haul)
    # For elog_rect, date is already in haul_sf (grouped by date during construction).
    # For all other sources, join date from the appropriate source dataset by haul_id.
    if (haul_catch_source == "elog_rect") {
      haul_sf <- haul_sf %>%
        dplyr::mutate(day_lbl = format(as.Date(date), "%d/%m"))
    } else {
      date_src <- if (haul_catch_source == "elog_trek") et
                  else if (haul_catch_source == "haul")  h
                  else                                    e   # "elog" or any other
      haul_dates <- date_src %>%
        dplyr::distinct(haul_id, date) %>%
        dplyr::group_by(haul_id) %>%
        dplyr::slice(1) %>%
        dplyr::ungroup()
      # Only join date if not already present (avoids date.x/date.y conflict)
      if (!"date" %in% names(haul_sf)) {
        haul_sf <- dplyr::left_join(haul_sf, haul_dates, by = "haul_id")
      }
      haul_sf <- haul_sf %>%
        dplyr::mutate(day_lbl = format(as.Date(date), "%d/%m"))
    }
    day_levels <- sort(unique(haul_sf$day_lbl))
    haul_sf <- dplyr::mutate(haul_sf,
                              day_lbl = factor(day_lbl, levels = day_levels))
    day_colours <- setNames(
      pal_day_pal(length(day_levels)),
      day_levels
    )
    # Map each trip to its first fishing day colour for route lines
    haul_trip_day <- if (haul_catch_source == "elog_trek") dplyr::distinct(et, trip_id, date)
                     else if (haul_catch_source == "haul")     dplyr::distinct(h,  trip_id, date)
                     else if (haul_catch_source == "elog_rect") haul_catch %>%
                       dplyr::mutate(trip_id = dplyr::first(settrip)) %>%
                       dplyr::distinct(trip_id, date)
                     else                                       dplyr::distinct(e,  trip_id, date)
    trip_colour_map <- haul_trip_day %>%
      dplyr::arrange(trip_id, date) %>%
      dplyr::group_by(trip_id) %>%
      dplyr::slice(1) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(day_lbl = format(date, "%d/%m"),
                    colour  = day_colours[day_lbl]) %>%
      dplyr::select(trip_id, colour) %>%
      dplyr::ungroup()
    p <- p +
      geom_sf(data = haul_sf,
              aes(size = weight_kg, fill = day_lbl),
              shape = 21, colour = "white", stroke = 0.3, alpha = 0.65) +
      scale_size_continuous(
        name   = if (lang == "nl") "Vangst (kg)" else "Catch (kg)",
        range  = c(2, 16),
        labels = scales::comma
      ) +
      scale_fill_manual(
        name   = if (lang == "nl") "Dag" else "Day",
        values = day_colours
      ) +
      guides(
        # Day colours: large filled squares for clear colour identification
        fill = guide_legend(
          nrow          = 1,
          order         = 1,
          override.aes  = list(shape = 22, size = 5, stroke = 0.3, colour = "white")
        ),
        # Catch weight: grey bubbles showing size scale
        size = guide_legend(
          nrow          = 1,
          order         = 2,
          override.aes  = list(fill = "grey60", colour = "white", stroke = 0.3)
        )
      )
  } else {
    # Fallback: plain points, one per unique position
    fb_src <- if (has_elog_trek) et else if (has_haul) h else e
    point_sf <- make_sf_points(fb_src)
    if ("haul_id" %in% names(point_sf)) {
      point_sf <- point_sf %>%
        dplyr::group_by(haul_id) %>%
        dplyr::slice(1) %>%
        dplyr::ungroup()
    } else {
      point_sf <- dplyr::distinct(point_sf, shoot_lon, shoot_lat, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
        dplyr::mutate(haul_id = dplyr::row_number())
    }
    p <- p +
      geom_sf(data = point_sf,
              aes(colour = factor(haul_id)),
              size = 2.5, alpha = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
      scale_colour_viridis_d(option = "turbo")
  }

  # Port markers (red dots + label)
  if (!is.null(ports_sf) && nrow(ports_sf) > 0) {
    p <- p +
      geom_sf(data = ports_sf,
              colour = "red", fill = "red",
              shape = 21, size = 4, stroke = 1.2) +
      geom_sf_text(data = ports_sf,
                   aes(label = port),
                   colour = "red", size = 2.8, fontface = "bold",
                   nudge_y = (ymax - ymin) * 0.025,
                   check_overlap = TRUE)
  }

  # Place names and ports
  p <- p + places_layer

  p <- p +
    labs(
      title    = glue("{paste(setvessel, collapse='/')} — {txt(\"plot_locs\")}"),
      subtitle = glue("{txt(\"plot_trip_lbl\")} {paste(settrip, collapse=', ')} | ",
                      "{format(report_start,'%d %b')} – {format(report_end,'%d %b %Y')}"),
```
